jokes.text: Good morning, Regular Person Lemon.
jokes.punchline: Overly formal, clinical way to address someone casually
jokes.explanation: Establishes Drew's clinical, overly formal character through an absurd way to address someone. Economical and character-specific.

jokes.text: Saturday is Valentine's Day? Norts!
jokes.punchline: Made-up exclamation 'Norts!' as expression of dismay
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz Lemon invented word expressing frustration. Very economical and character-specific, became quotable.

jokes.text: You sound so sexy when you say that. Say it again.
jokes.setup: Liz just said the made-up word 'Norts!'
jokes.punchline: Drew finds this absurd outburst sexy
jokes.explanation: Good character work - Drew's clinical mind finding weird things attractive. Solid structure and economy.

jokes.text: These McFlurries are amazing. I know-- the soft swirl of vanilla and the hard crunch of candy and cookies. You'd think they'd fight each other, but no. Together, they are perfecto.
jokes.punchline: Overly poetic, romantic description of fast food
jokes.explanation: Nice juxtaposition of romantic language applied to McDonald's. Sets up the high-low contrast theme.

jokes.text: Don't tell me you're one of those convenient Catholics that only goes to church every Sunday.
jokes.punchline: Calling weekly church attendance 'convenient' Catholicism
jokes.explanation: Brilliant inversion of 'convenient Catholic' trope. Perfect economy and unexpected twist on familiar phrase.

jokes.text: Do I? I wouldn't have this job if it weren't for the mouth on my back.
jokes.setup: Tracy asking Kenneth about disabled hiring practices
jokes.punchline: Kenneth casually mentioning he has a mouth on his back
jokes.explanation: Peak 30 Rock absurdist humor. Completely unexpected, delivered deadpan, adds to Kenneth's mysterious nature.

jokes.text: I'm just funning, it's all sewed up.
jokes.punchline: Tracy's malapropism mixing 'funning' and 'sewed up'
jokes.explanation: Typical Tracy wordplay. Character-appropriate but not particularly clever or memorable.

jokes.text: Is everything okay? Because now, I smell burnt plastic.
jokes.punchline: She can smell something's wrong even though she can't see
jokes.explanation: Good sensory-based humor playing on her blindness without being cruel. Suggests visual chaos happening.

jokes.text: There is handsomeness involved.
jokes.setup: Jack asking why Liz is nervous about her date
jokes.punchline: Treating handsomeness like a medical condition
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz awkwardness - clinical way to describe attraction. Very economical and character-specific.

jokes.text: Slut buster? / No, not a ball player.
jokes.setup: Jack running through potential issues with Liz's date
jokes.punchline: Liz mishearing 'slugbuster' as 'slut buster'
jokes.explanation: Good rapid-fire dialogue with misunderstanding payoff. Shows Liz's mind going to wrong places.

jokes.text: If I had those knockers, I'd be thanking God, too.
jokes.punchline: Reducing her faith to gratitude for physical attributes
jokes.explanation: Classic inappropriate Jack comment. Character-consistent but relies on crude humor.

jokes.text: I don't know. Have you ever put a donut in the microwave?
jokes.punchline: Liz's simple, relatable food pleasure
jokes.explanation: Perfect character contrast - Jack's pretension vs Liz's simple pleasures. Extremely economical and relatable.

jokes.text: Thanks. I found it at Dunkin... wait a minute.
jokes.punchline: Liz realizing Jack's fake enthusiasm demonstration
jokes.explanation: Good beat showing Liz catching on to Jack's manipulation. Nice timing and character awareness.

jokes.text: Ken, you need to snap out of it and ask out that sexy Ms. Magoo.
jokes.setup: Tracy encouraging Kenneth with the blind woman
jokes.punchline: Combining 'sexy' with cartoon reference to Mr. Magoo
jokes.explanation: Clever reference mixing attraction with cartoon character known for poor vision. Economical.

jokes.text: Okay, there is something about Jennifer that just makes me all carsick inside.
jokes.setup: Kenneth describing his feelings for Jennifer
jokes.punchline: Describing attraction as 'carsick inside'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth-esque description of romance. Innocent and weird in character-specific way.

jokes.text: Well, cotton and fiddles. I enjoy your smile.
jokes.punchline: Bizarre country exclamation followed by formal compliment
jokes.explanation: Wonderful Kenneth dialogue mixing rural expressions with formal politeness. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Yes, indeedy corncobs?
jokes.setup: Kenneth asking Jennifer on a date
jokes.punchline: Jennifer's equally bizarre country expression
jokes.explanation: Nice matching of Kenneth's linguistic style. Shows they're compatible through shared weird speech.

jokes.text: It's a Valentine's date-li-doo!
jokes.punchline: Adding nonsense suffix to make 'date-li-doo'
jokes.explanation: Kenneth's innocent excitement expressed through made-up word. Character-appropriate but not exceptional.

jokes.text: It's my own recipe where I use cheddar cheese instead of water.
jokes.punchline: Revealing she replaced water with cheese in the recipe
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz Lemon food disaster. Absurd but believable for her character. Great revelation structure.

jokes.text: Well, it's just gotten so small that I kind of want to see if it disappears.
jokes.punchline: Liz's scientific curiosity about decay
jokes.explanation: Great character detail showing Liz's weird domestic habits and procrastination rationalized as curiosity.

jokes.text: Okay. Well, I guess we just... jumped ahead to date four. It's not the good one, either.
jokes.punchline: Clinical assessment of which breast is worse
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy with Liz's self-deprecating honesty. Body humor handled with character specificity.

jokes.text: Jonathan... our Jonathan, who art in the office hallowed be my reservation. If you are able... hold my table at Plunder as we will not be there by seven.
jokes.punchline: Reciting Lord's Prayer structure for restaurant reservation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant parody structure applying religious prayer to mundane request. Shows Jack's dramatic flair.

jokes.text: Oh, cheese stew. What was I thinking?
jokes.punchline: Delayed recognition of obvious problem
jokes.explanation: Good callback to her absurd recipe. Shows character's slow realization of her mistakes.

jokes.text: The draft made the door fly open! I know. It's not a big deal. So, we skipped from date four to date... 20.
jokes.setup: Kitchen window causes door to open
jokes.punchline: Liz's dating timeline accelerated by accident
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of physical comedy with callback to date progression system.

jokes.text: Mandy? Is that, like, a guy friend, like Mandy Patinkin?
jokes.punchline: Liz's innocent assumption about gender-ambiguous name
jokes.explanation: Nice pop culture reference showing Liz's naivety. Reasonable structure but not exceptional.

jokes.text: You will see her. You will say she's cute. But let me just say that she is... complicated.
jokes.punchline: Ominous description of child as 'complicated'
jokes.explanation: Good setup for child character. Clinical way to describe problematic kid fits Drew's character.

jokes.text: Mom's keying your car.
jokes.punchline: Casual announcement of vandalism
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy - immediately establishes child's matter-of-fact approach to chaos. Dark but effective.

jokes.text: Because, honey, you keep setting fire to things.
jokes.setup: Bethany asking why she can't keep her purse
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of child arsonist tendencies
jokes.explanation: Dark parenting humor delivered matter-of-factly. Explains the 'complicated' comment effectively.

jokes.text: Okay, here's how this is going to go. We're going to sit here in silence for about three minutes. Then, I'm going to take my girlfriend to Plunder where the Pope himself could not get a table.
jokes.punchline: Treating confession like business meeting, bragging about restaurant access
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack arrogance - treating religious sacrament like inconvenient meeting. Good character integration.

jokes.text: I didn't know it was a French restaurant. Yes, I found it on my favorite website StopShowingOff.com.
jokes.setup: Kenneth bringing Jennifer to fancy restaurant
jokes.punchline: Website name that contradicts his current behavior
jokes.explanation: Excellent irony - Kenneth using anti-pretentious website to find pretentious restaurant. Good economy.

jokes.text: I have faith... in things I can see and buy and deregulate. Capitalism is my religion.
jokes.punchline: Jack's corporate philosophy as religious doctrine
jokes.explanation: Perfect character summation - Jack's worldview in one line. Highly quotable and character-defining.

jokes.text: I hit my mother with a car... possibly by accident.
jokes.punchline: Uncertainty about whether matricide was intentional
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy escalation. The 'possibly' makes it even more disturbing and funny. Perfect economy.

jokes.text: And... I may have sodomized our former Vice President while under the influence of some weapons-grade narcotics.
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific and shocking admission
jokes.explanation: Peak escalation of Jack's confessions. Shocking but fits the show's boundaries. Less economy than previous.

jokes.text: I need backup! Harvard did not prepare me for this.
jokes.punchline: Priest calling for help, citing inadequate education
jokes.explanation: Great reaction to Jack's confessions. The Harvard reference adds specificity and humor.

jokes.text: Blue is for showing your boobs. Purple is for doing stuff over your clothes. Black is for doing stuff...
jokes.punchline: Innocent description of sexual activity tracking system
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about children's exposure to adult content. Cringe-inducing but fits the show's style.

jokes.text: Wow, he does not sound good live.
jokes.setup: Michael McDonald performing at fancy restaurant
jokes.punchline: Jack's blunt assessment of live performance
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about celebrity performances. Economical and relatable.

jokes.text: You know what your problem is, Jack? You intellectualize everything with your big head. Well, you have big boobs.
jokes.setup: Elisa criticizing Jack's approach to faith
jokes.punchline: Jack's juvenile comeback about her physical attributes
jokes.explanation: Shows Jack reverting to immature behavior when challenged. Character-appropriate but crude.

jokes.text: the patron saint of judgmental statues.
jokes.punchline: Making up specific patronage for judging people
jokes.explanation: Clever invention of specific saint duty. Very economical and memorable phrase.

jokes.text: What kind of God would let that happen?
jokes.punchline: Jack questioning God's existence over personal romantic failure
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Jack's narcissism making personal problems theological. Solid structure.

jokes.text: I'm sorry... is this, like, a 'Sixth Sense' thing? Should I bring a place setting for your friend?
jokes.setup: Drew talking to his dying mother
jokes.punchline: Nurse thinking Liz might be imaginary/ghost
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference applied to awkward social situation. Well-structured with clear setup.

jokes.text: The woman he thinks is his sister is really his mother. I'm his grandmother.
jokes.setup: Dying woman asking Liz to deliver message
jokes.punchline: Revealing shocking family secret on deathbed
jokes.explanation: Classic soap opera twist delivered in medical setting. Dark but fits the episode's escalating chaos.

jokes.text: Kenneth, why do you suddenly sound white?
jokes.punchline: Direct observation about racial vocal patterns
jokes.explanation: Bold racial humor handled through character observation. Economical and direct.

jokes.text: And when I'm with you, my heart jumps like a frog on July asphalt right before it dies.
jokes.punchline: Romantic metaphor ending in death
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth poetry - starts romantic but ends morbidly. Brilliant character-specific imagery that's highly quotable.

jokes.text: That is cold, blind lady! Oh, and by the way... you are not so attractive, yourself. No, I'm... I'm pretty sure I'm hot. Yes, you are. I tried.
jokes.setup: Jennifer rejecting Kenneth after feeling his face
jokes.punchline: Tracy's attempted support failing immediately
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Tracy's inability to lie convincingly. Character-appropriate failure.

jokes.text: You know, my mom did just die. Funny story.
jokes.punchline: Calling maternal death a 'funny story'
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy with perfect economy. The clinical detachment makes it funnier and more disturbing.

jokes.text: You know where I found it? In the collection plate. It was a sign.
jokes.punchline: Finding religious significance in church theft
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack logic - finding divine meaning in his own theft. Great character consistency and irony.

jokes.text: I believe that we were reunited by the most successful capitalist enterprise of the last hundred years despite the McLean Deluxe or the McEmu.
jokes.punchline: Attributing divine intervention to McDonald's corporation and referencing failed menu items
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jack monologue combining corporate worship with romantic reunion. Perfect character voice and obscure McDonald's references.

jokes.text: Boy, I hope that guy's not planning to kill and eat her.
jokes.setup: Jack and Elisa watching romantic couple
jokes.punchline: Jack's dark interpretation of romance
jokes.explanation: Classic Jack cynicism about romance. Character-appropriate dark turn but not exceptional.
